data.title: WeShop skyrockets as its shoppers-become-shareholders model resonates
data.description:
WeShop Holdings Limited (WSHP) is up more than 100% this week since debuting on the Nasdaq in a direct listing of shares.

The UK-based social commerce platform allows consumers to earn shares in the company through their shopping activity and referrals.  Users earn WePoints for shopping or making referrals, which can then be converted into shares of WeShop (WSHP).

The London-based company's concept emerged in the early 2010s, led by Chairman Richard Griffiths, who envisioned a disruptive model that turned shopping and referrals into real equity for users.  Griffith and the other founders believe traditional online shopping platforms concentrate wealth at the top, leaving the actual customers unrewarded.

Looking ahead, the company plans to launch its app in the U.S. market, providing shoppers what is says will be a seamless shopping experience along with the opportunity to create ownership. In the UK, WeShop (WSHP) already partners with major retailers such as John Lewis, eBay, Selfridges, ASOS, Expedia, British Airways, TEMU, Shein, and many more. The company has completed over $140 million of sales in its UK pilot.

Trading on WeShop (WSHP) opened at $20.02 on November 14. In Wednesday morning trading, shares were up 48% to $49.00.

data.title: Netflix taking bid for WBD 'very seriously' amid tough antitrust implications - CNBC's Faber
data.description:
CNBC's David Faber said Wednesday that Netflix (NFLX) is considering the offer to buy Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) "very seriously" as the deadline for the first round of bids nears.  

"In my mind, they (Netflix) are seen as the key. If they want to own Warner Bros., they can," Faber said on CNBC, while implying that the streaming giant does not necessarily have to buy WBD, but they could if they want to. 

"The antitrust implication of owning HBO along with Netflix—that's a tough one," Faber added.

He also said that the deal could either hurt Netflix's multiple, or it could be accretive up to "a fairly large number."

For Comcast (CMCSA), Faber said the deal would be very difficult, resulting in a very complicated transaction, while also raising doubts that its bid could compete with Paramount's (PSKY).

Recent media reports said WBD wants Paramount to offer $30 per share and buy out the entire company in a +$74B deal. Paramount initially made three bids, which were swiftly rejected. 

A report by Variety also suggested the David Ellison-led company, along with a consortium of Arab sovereign wealth funds, is preparing to submit a $71B offer for WBD, which Paramount has denied.

data.title: Microsoft reveals 'compelling vision' for future of enterprise AI: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Microsoft's (MSFT) Ignite conference is being held in San Francisco this week, and the analysts at J.P. Morgan said the software and cloud giant revealed a compelling case for its version of enterprise AI and what it calls "Frontier Firms."

"Microsoft further defines its vision for the future of AI as it sees the technology evolving from concept and experimentation and toward implementation and utilization, with AI becoming ubiquitous across organizations, necessitating effective, secure, and compliant orchestration and observability as multi-agent ecosystems emerge," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Mark Murphy, in a Wednesday investor note.

J.P. Morgan reiterated its Overweight rating and $575 price target on Microsoft.

Citing an IDC study that expects as many as 1.3B AI agents will be deployed by 2028, Microsoft announced its Agent 365, which will help clients deploy and scale agents across a business. Microsoft also discussed the importance of an Intelligence Layer, which can help unify structured and unstructured data for utilization by said agents.

Microsoft announced an App Builder for M365 Copilot as well as "software is evolving from being made for people to by people" thanks to the rise of AI-powered coding processes.

"With App Builder, users can create an app within a matter of minutes through the combination of natural language and the intelligence layer within Microsoft's platform that provides business-specific context to AI apps," Murphy noted. "This complements Microsoft's GitHub Copilot family of products, helping provide development tools to users from the technical to the non-technical."

Finally, J.P. Morgan highlighted the new partnership between Microsoft, Nvidia (NVDA) and Anthropic (ANTHRO). As part of the deal, Nvidia and Microsoft are committing to invest up to $10B and up to $5B, respectively, in Anthropic. Anthropic also said it has committed to spend $30B on Microsoft's Azure compute capacity and to contract additional compute capacity up to one gigawatt.

"While MSFT continues to encounter some AI capacity constraints modestly restricting Azure growth, we see it planting the longer-term seeds for success across Security, Teams, Power Apps and now the forward-looking OpenAI/ChatGPT investments," Murphy added. "In addition, limited headcount growth shows the commitment to efficiency and alignment of OpEx to the new environment."

data.title: Truist names Microsoft veteran Pascal Belaud as chief AI & data officer
data.description:
Truist Financial Corp. (TFC) appointed Microsoft (MSFT) veteran Pascal Belaud as the bank's chief AI & data officer, starting Nov. 24, it said on Wednesday.

Belaud will be tasked to shape and vision and execution of Truist's (TFC) AI, data and advanced automation strategy across the company. He will report to Truist Chief Information Officer Steve Hagerman.

During its Q3 earnings call last month, Truist (TFC) CEO William Rogers highlighted the company's focus on AI-driven branch investments and further digital engagement.

Belaud has over 25 years at Microsoft (MSFT), having held a number of leadership roles in Microsoft's US financial sector, across AI, data and data insights, customer success, analytics, cloud, machine learning and predictive analysis.

"I believe we have tremendous opportunities to strategically and responsibly harness the full power of AI and data to transform client and teammate experiences," Belaud said in a statement.

data.title: Boeing, Airbus trade surprises at Dubai Airshow as Emirates, Flydubai drive major orders
data.description:
This year’s Dubai Airshow delivered a mix of twists and unexpected wins as Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) and Boeing (BA) sparred for prominence, with communist China’s Comac stepping onto the global stage in a more serious way than ever before.

Emirates kicked off the event with its headline purchase: 65 more Boeing (BA) 777X jets valued at $38 billion. The decision to renew its commitment to Boeing’s (BA) delayed flagship surprised some observers, given recent schedule setbacks. Still, analysts noted the Dubai-based carrier is already planning for the 2030s as it prepares to retire some of its massive A380s.

 

 

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) answered on day two with its own stunner, securing an order from Flydubai, long viewed as a Boeing (BA) loyalist thanks to its all-737 fleet. The European planemaker’s aircraft would give the low-cost carrier more flexibility to open thinner long-haul routes, according to industry specialists.

But Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) again failed to land the prize it covets most: a major A350-1000 order from Emirates. President Tim Clark said he won’t commit until he sees more from the jet’s engines, echoing the same stalemate seen at the previous Dubai show.

Both manufacturers chalked up wins midweek. Boeing (BA) locked in a Flydubai agreement for 75 more 737s plus options for an equal number, while Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) secured a top-up order for Emirates’ A350-900s. Airbus also signed Buraq Air of Libya as a new customer for 10 A320-family aircraft.

The rapid cadence of announcements captured the high-pressure nature of Dubai’s show, now one of the industry’s main arenas for blockbuster deals. Negotiations often come together overnight or collapse at the last minute, and many signings represent early commitments rather than final contracts. The surge of demand only adds pressure to manufacturers still struggling with long backlogs and supply-chain constraints.

Dubai’s role as a global aviation crossroads, anchored by Emirates’ long-haul network, has turned Gulf carriers into essential customers for Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) and Boeing (BA), rivaling the massive order volumes coming from China and India. While the duopoly still dominates the widebody market, competition is emerging elsewhere.

China’s Comac used the show to promote its C919 outside Asia for the first time, flying the narrow-body jet alongside the Airbus A350 and Boeing 777. The aircraft resembles the A320 in size and profile, though it still lacks certification in Europe and the United States. Even so, its appearance underscored that the long-standing two-player rivalry could be entering a new era.

data.title: Griffon outlines $2.5B revenue and $580M–$600M EBITDA targets for 2026 while strengthening capital returns
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Griffon Corporation (GFF) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ronald Kramer reported, "We're very pleased with our results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year, particularly in light of the challenging macroeconomic environment. The continued strong performance from our Home & Building Products, or HBP segment, combined with the meaningful profitability improvements in our Consumer and Professional Products segment, CPP, underscores the strength of our portfolio and the operational discipline."
  • Kramer highlighted HBP segment revenue at $1.6 billion, with an EBITDA margin of 31.2%. He detailed product innovation awards for Clopay, including the Best in Show award for VertiStack Avante garage door and a 2025 Partner of the Year Award from Home Depot.
  • The CPP segment experienced a 10% revenue decline to $936 million because of weak consumer demand and disrupted U.S. ordering due to tariffs. However, segment EBITDA rose 18% and margin increased by over 200 basis points, attributed to global sourcing and an asset-light business model.
  • Kramer stated, "We repurchased 1.9 million shares at an average price of $70.99. Since April 2023 and through September 30, 2025, our share repurchases totaled 10.8 million shares...for a total of $560 million or an average of $51.79 per share."
  • The Board authorized a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per share, a 22% increase over the prior quarter, marking the 57th consecutive quarterly dividend.
  • CFO Brian Harris said, "Fourth quarter revenue of $662 million and adjusted EBITDA of $138 million were both consistent with the prior year. Segment adjusted EBITDA and EBITDA margin for the quarter was $154 million and 23.2%, respectively, both consistent with the prior year."

Outlook

  • Harris provided 2026 guidance: "We expect Griffon fiscal year 2026 revenue to be consistent with 2025 at $2.5 billion and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $580 million to $600 million, excluding unallocated costs of $58 million."
  • HBP and CPP revenue in 2026 are anticipated to match 2025 levels. HBP EBITDA margin is projected to remain above 30%, and CPP margin is expected at approximately 10%.
  • Free cash flow for 2026, including $60 million in capital expenditures, is expected to exceed net income. Interest expense is forecast at $93 million, and the normalized tax rate at 28%.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $662 million; gross profit on a GAAP basis was $276 million compared to $263 million in the prior year quarter.
  • Adjusted net income was $71 million or $1.54 per share compared to $71 million or $1.47 per share in the prior year.
  • Net capital expenditures were $12 million, and depreciation and amortization totaled $15.9 million for the quarter.
  • HBP revenue increased 3% over the prior year quarter owing to favorable price and mix. CPP revenue decreased 4%, primarily due to reduced volume in the U.S. and U.K., partially offset by gains in Australia and Canada.
  • Free cash flow for 2025 reached $323 million, with $116 million in debt reduction and share repurchases totaling $135 million for the year.

Q&A

  • Collin Verron, Deutsche Bank: Asked about HBP margin drivers and upcoming trends. Harris explained, "We have varying mix in product from time to time...Volume was relatively flat with commercial being slightly up and residential being slightly down."
  • Verron inquired about seasonality and guide weightings. Harris stated a "slight 1% or 2% decrease in the first half...and the opposite pickup in the second half of the year."
  • Timothy Wojs, Baird: Queried about CPP variance and tariff strategies. Harris responded that favorable pricing mix and slightly better volume drove results, and that global supply chain and cost management would continue to offset tariffs.
  • Bob Labick, CJS Securities: Asked about competitive landscape and CPP pricing. Kramer indicated no pricing discipline issues among competitors and stated, "brands matter and quality matters."
  • Sam Darkatsh, Raymond James: Questioned retailer inventory and capital allocation signals. Kramer said, "we see a very balanced, a very strong business that continues to gain market share" and clarified that dividend increases and share repurchases could be pursued concurrently.
  • Julio Romero, Sidoti: Asked about product line performance and margin headroom. Harris noted strength in Australia and continued supply chain benefits, while Kramer emphasized the 15% long-term margin target for CPP.
  • Jeffrey Stevenson, Loop Capital: Asked about residential garage door demand and China exposure. Harris reported consistent volume at the high end, with global supply chain flexibility mitigating tariff risks.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on margin sustainability, competitive positioning, tariff impacts, and capital returns, indicating a neutral to slightly positive tone, with targeted questions on guidance and operational strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, repeatedly emphasizing operational discipline, innovation, and capital return strategies. Kramer described the outlook as "bullish" and Harris used phrases such as "we are well positioned" and "we are confident in our ability to continue to generate strong financial performance."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained optimistic but was more explicit in addressing seasonality, margin drivers, and capital allocation strategy. Analyst questioning maintained a practical tone, pressing for clarity on macro headwinds, inventory, and guidance assumptions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Fiscal 2026 revenue and EBITDA guidance were reaffirmed at $2.5 billion and $580–$600 million, respectively, matching the previous quarter’s revised outlook.
  • Dividend increased 22% over the prior quarter; share repurchases continued but at a slightly reduced pace compared to earlier in the year.
  • Discussion of HBP and CPP margin targets remained consistent, with continued focus on innovation, global sourcing, and cost control.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on consumer demand, tariff impacts, and margin expansion, but this quarter included more granular discussion of seasonality and inventory normalization.
  • Management’s confidence in long-term targets and capital allocation remained high and was reiterated strongly in the Q4 call.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited weak consumer demand in North America and the U.K. and disrupted U.S. ordering patterns due to tariffs as persistent challenges.
  • Tariff policy and consumer normalization were highlighted as key variables for 2026, with strategies in place to leverage global supply chain flexibility.
  • Weather-related volatility and continued macroeconomic uncertainty, particularly in housing and consumer markets, were acknowledged as ongoing risks.
  • Analysts raised concerns about inventory normalization, tariff exposure, and the sustainability of recent margin improvements.

Final Takeaway

Griffon Corporation closed fiscal 2025 emphasizing strong operational performance in challenging conditions, highlighted by robust capital returns, margin improvement, and product innovation. Management projects flat revenue and EBITDA for 2026, with a focus on sustaining high HBP margins and advancing CPP profitability through global sourcing and supply chain flexibility. Shareholder value remains a priority through increased dividends, share repurchases, and ongoing debt reduction, as management maintains confidence in the company’s long-term strategic outlook and resilience against macroeconomic headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Kraken files confidentially for proposed IPO
data.description:
Payward, which operates under the Kraken (KRAKEN) name, confidentially filed a draft registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a proposed initial public offering of common stock, the company said on Wednesday.

The number of shares to be offered and price range for the proposed IPO haven't yet been determined. That will depend on the demand for the shares in the crypto exchange operator.

The company is tapping the public equities market after several crypto IPOs showed strong demand earlier this year — including stablecoin company Circle Internet (CRCL), Bullish (BLSH), and Gemini Space Station (GEMI). However, bitcoin (BTC-USD), the world's biggest cryptocurrency by market cap, has retreated by more than 25% from its all-time high of ~$126.2K reached on Oct. 6, which may make a crypto exchange stock less appealing to investors.

The IPO is expected to occur after the SEC completes its review process, subject to market and other conditions, the company said.

In September, Kraken (KRAKEN) closed a $500M capital raise that valued it at $15B. It then said it was in advanced talks for new funding that could value it at about $20B.

data.title: Atkore gains on report it's exploring a potential sale
data.description:
Atkore Inc. (ATKR) rose 3% after a report that it's exploring a potential sale amid pressure from an activist investor.

Atkore could give an update on its sales process when it reports results on Thursday, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The electrical manufacturing company may see interest from both strategic and private equity buyers.

Atkore declined to comment to Bloomberg.

The report comes after Bloomberg reported in late September that activist investor Irenic Capital Management had taken a stake and  wants the company to explore a sale.

data.title: Novo job cuts push Denmark to record worst year for layoff since 2020: Bloomberg News
data.description:
Denmark is on track to record its worst year for job losses since the pandemic amid a massive restructuring effort by the country’s largest company, Novo Nordisk (NVO), which announced 5,000 layoffs in its home nation last September, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

Citing government data, the news outlet reported that Denmark has recorded more than 13,300 layoff notices from roughly 250 companies for the Jan–Oct period, its worst year for job losses since 2020 when the Nordic country witnessed nearly 22,000 layoffs amid social curbs.

More than 50% of layoff notices for 2025 have been recorded in September, when Novo Nordisk (NVO) initiated a large-scale cost-cutting drive involving about 9,000 job cuts globally, including 5,000 layoffs in Denmark.

According to the government tally for the month, Gladsaxe municipality, where the weight loss drugmaker is based, has recorded over 2,400 job cuts at companies with more than 100 employees.

Ballerup, where Novo (NVO) operates its R&D function in Denmark, has recorded roughly 800 redundancies, while Hillerod and Kalundborg, two main manufacturing hubs for the company, have posted more than 450 reductions.
